top_mentions.text: New study finds microplastics in 90% of table salt brands worldwide. We're literally eating the ocean's plastic problem.
AI-powered satellite monitoring could finally give us a real-time picture of ocean plastic distribution. This changes the cleanup calculus entirely.
The Global Plastics Treaty negotiations just wrapped with no binding reduction targets. Again. How many rounds before we admit voluntary commitments don't work?
Researchers mapped microplastic concentration in the Pacific using computer vision on satellite data. Density is 40% higher than previous estimates in some zones.
The Ocean Cleanup just announced they've removed 50 million kg of trash from the Great Pacific Garbage Patch. But scientists warn the inflow rate still exceeds cleanup capacity.
